title: The Dungeon of D - A Small Box Dungeon Crawl Done Right
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=9TvuNO-fnXM

Daniel (@dungeondive) reviews Jack D's *Dungeon of D*, a free print-and-play single-deck dungeon crawl — he recommends ordering the cards from Printer Studio for under twenty dollars, because the linen-finish stock is genuinely better than the retail Adventures of D 2nd Edition box. Jack D, he argues, is the king of multi-use cards: every card in the Power deck does at least five jobs.

Each card simultaneously functions as hit points (your hand), stat-check power totals (with bonus draws when you lead with the matching stat), wandering-monster lookup (the central monster number), and inventory items (the bottom half flips for potions, items, or special gear). The four monster cards rotate to show four different enemies each, scaled by floor — so 16 monster faces from four physical cards.

Structure: eight floors, eight rooms each, persistent layout once revealed. The mystic portal between floors. The ice dragon and the Amulet of Diogor wait at floor eight. Rooms exhaust on use (flipped face-down), and many trigger different effects depending on how many rooms you've already revealed. Daniel rates the dungeon-exploration feel above *Dungeoneer* for solo play.

----

Jack D builds whole adventures from one deck by exhausting card real estate the way a Swiss army knife exhausts handle space. Does that economy still feel clever after multiple plays, or does the bookkeeping wear?
